enonce.texte:
Read the flyer below and answer the questions that follow.

🌈 FUN ACTIVITIES CLUB 🌈

Join us for exciting recreational activities!

  • ⚽ Football: Every Saturday at 3 PM
  • 🏀 Basketball: Every Tuesday and Thursday at 4 PM
  • 🎵 Music and Dance: Every Friday at 5 PM
  • 🎨 Arts and Crafts: Every Wednesday at 3 PM
  • 📚 Reading Club: Every Monday at 4 PM

Location: Community Youth Centre

Contact: 699-555-1234 for more information

All activities are free for members!
images: A colourful flyer showing children participating in various recreational activities: football, basketball, music, arts and crafts, and reading.

questions.texte: What is the name of the club?
List three recreational activities mentioned in the flyer.
questions.reponse: Football, Basketball, Music and Dance, Arts and Crafts, Reading Club (any three)

questions.texte: When does the Music and Dance activity take place?
Where is the club located?
Explain why joining a club like the Fun Activities Club can be beneficial for young people.
questions.reponse: Joining a club like Fun Activities Club is beneficial because it allows young people to learn new skills, make new friends, stay physically active, develop creativity, and spend their free time in a productive and positive way.

intitule: Grammar Practice: Phrasal Verbs with 'put', 'look', and 'give'

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct phrasal verb from the list: put on, put off, put up with, look after, look for, look forward to, give up, give back, give in.

questions.texte: I need to _______ my sports shoes before the game.
She _______ her younger brother while their parents were away.
They decided to _______ the meeting until next week.
I really _______ spending time with my friends.
She wants to _______ smoking for her health.
I can't _______ the noise in this neighbourhood.

intitule: Writing a Dialogue about Recreational Activities

enonce.texte: Write a dialogue between two friends discussing their favourite recreational activities. Use at least three phrasal verbs from the lesson ('put on', 'look after', 'give up', etc.) in the dialogue.

questions.texte: Write your dialogue here.
questions.reponse:
Sample answer:

Jean: Hi Marie! What do you usually do in your free time?

Marie: Hi Jean! I love playing football with my friends. I always put on my sports shoes and go to the field. What about you?

Jean: I enjoy swimming. I go to the pool every Saturday.

Marie: That sounds great! I used to swim, but I gave up because I didn't have enough time.

Jean: You should try again! I look forward to swimming every week because it helps me relax.

Marie: I might join you one day. I also like reading books.

Jean: That's a good hobby too. I need to look for more time to read.

Marie: We should do activities together more often!

intitule: Creating a Flyer for a Recreational Activity

enonce.texte: Create a flyer to promote a recreational activity at your school. Include the following: activity name, day, time, location, who can join, and contact information. Use at least three phrasal verbs in your flyer.

questions.texte: Write your flyer here.
questions.reponse:
Sample answer:

🌟 SCHOOL BASKETBALL TOURNAMENT 🌟

Do you love sports?

Come and put on your best sports gear for our annual basketball tournament!

  • Day: Saturday, December 15
  • Time: 9:00 AM
  • Location: School Sports Hall
  • Who can join: All students!

We look forward to seeing you there. Don't give up on your dream of being a champion!

Contact: Sports Club President at 699-555-1234

enonce.texte: Match the phrasal verb with its correct meaning.

questions.texte: put on
questions.reponse: to wear or dress oneself in something

questions.texte: look for
give up
look after
questions.reponse: to take care of someone or something

questions.texte: give back
questions.reponse: to return something to the owner

questions.texte: look forward to
questions.reponse: to be excited about something that will happen

intitule: Completing a Dialogue about Recreational Activities

enonce.texte: Complete the following dialogue with the correct phrasal verbs from the list: put on, look after, give up, look for, look forward to, give back.

questions.texte:
A: Hi! Are you going to the football game this afternoon?

B: Yes, I am. I need to _______ my uniform first.

A: Great! I really _______ playing football with you.

B: Me too! I had to _______ playing last month because I was injured.

A: Oh, I'm sorry. I hope you're feeling better now.

B: Yes, I am. I've been trying to _______ my health.

A: That's good. Oh, I forgot to _______ your ball after last week's game. Sorry!

B: No problem. Just bring it today.
questions.reponse: put on, look forward to, give up, look after, give back
